Location: Metro Manila. | Camille Cervantes is both a design-thinker and a systems-thinker, allowing her to be both creative in problem solving, and fast in learning and adapting. Even as she found her way into the tech industry, Cam is also a licensed Landscape Architect, a consulting creative director, and freelances as a film production designer. 

She was the lead for Creatives and Performance Marketing for the e-grocery platform Rappit (formerly PureGo), a joint-venture between 917Ventures and Puregold, having a pivotal role in the company's rebrand and its marketing campaigns. Afterwards, she was absorbed into Globe's 917Ventures' Ideation and Acceleration Group, working across different industries and verticals, focusing on growth with data-driven, tech-first innovations.

She was also the Lead Graphic Designer, Set Designer, and Set Decorator for the first season of the US TV Series, "Almost Paradise" by Electric Entertainment and ABS-CBN International, under the production design team of Bigbulb Design Lab, Inc. A Creative Director for Bigbulb's Branding Department, she works with various clients for ideations, activations, film, TVCs, and product development (Drag Den PH, Ben & Ben, discovery+, HBO GO, among others).

As a visual artist that primarily works with acrylic, Cervantes similarly explores the use of pen/ink, digital painting, spray paint, and photo manipulation in her works. In July 2019, her works were under the "DIOS (Among Us)" group exhibit held at the Arbilo Galerie (Paris, France). She was also a finalist in the 2nd season of Art Battle Manila in FringeMNL 2017, and a featured mural artist for the city-wide Makati Makisalo festival in FringeMNL 2018. | Metro Manila

candidates.notes:
Discovered from low-cost search seed acquisition. Query: __past_company_only__. Source: harvest_profile_search. Location: Quezon City, National Capital Region, Philippines. | Healthcare for All and Cities for People

I am a designer at heart and find joy in reconfiguring systems so that wellbeing is built into the way we live. As a Special Ops Analyst on MedGrocer’s Population Health team, I lead innovation and capacity-building initiatives that strengthen healthcare for over 20,000 patients, testing first-of-kind models that inform the future of healthcare delivery in the country. 

At Duke University and Duke Kunshan University, I specialized in computational research across labs in U.S. and China that aim to reduce inequality in the Global South. These projects spanned co-developing antibiotic alternatives to combat drug-resistant infections, examining the effects of lesser studied product additives on coastal populations, and evaluating the impact of digital health tools. 

Beyond the clinical, I see wellbeing as a relational practice that emerges in the co-creation of space and culture. At Branch Out for Justice, I interviewed feminist leaders to document movement-building across Latin America. As the Lead of Young Urbanists Philippines, I implement platforms and programming that empower youth to advocate for inclusive planning education and youth-led tactical interventions within their cities. 

Across organizational, biological, and urban systems, I see capacity-building—and by extension, self-making—as the art of understanding disparate parts and rewiring them into interconnected strengths, by design. | Quezon City, National Capital Region, Philippines

Source account: harvest_company_employees. | Jonathan Lachman is the founding head of operations at Thinking Machines Lab, a frontier AI research and product company that is working to make AI broadly useful and understandable through solid technical foundations, open science, and practical applications. Previously, Jonathan was the head of special projects at OpenAI, where he supported a variety of company-wide strategic initiatives and served on OpenAI’s senior leadership team. Prior to joining OpenAI, Jonathan was on the management teams of Blend (NYSE:BLND) and Leap Motion. From 2014-2017, Jonathan was the senior Obama White House budget official for defense, intelligence, foreign policy, and veterans programs, and represented the Office of Management and Budget on the National Security Council's Deputies Committee. Prior to joining the White House, he served as chief of staff to Deputy Secretaries of Defense Christine Fox and Bob Work, and was a longstanding senior advisor to the late Secretary of Defense Ash Carter. For his contributions to national security, Jonathan received the Defense Department's Distinguished Public Service Award. Jonathan served on the Biden-Harris Transition Team and is a past term member of the Council on Foreign Relations. A graduate of Princeton and Harvard Kennedy School, he lives in San Francisco. | San Francisco, California, United States

(5) The company LinkedIn URL (105913171) for the matched experience should be verified against the canonical slug 'thinkingmachinesai' — a mismatch is possible. The structured company match exists, but the overall profile is implausible and likely a fabricated or spam account requiring manual review.

evidence.summary: LinkedIn membership review flagged الشيخ محمد علي الخليفة for manual review. | The profile matches the target company (Thinking Machines Lab) via a structured LinkedIn experience entry with the correct company URL, but the profile content is clearly implausible as a legitimate employee. The job title in the matched experience is 'الرياض' (a city name, not a job title), the description is also just 'الرياض', and the skills field contains spiritual healing services rather than any technical competencies. The profile is located in Cairo, Egypt, and the headline and summary are promotional text for spiritual healing services. This is a classic spam/fake profile that has attached itself to a legitimate company's LinkedIn page, likely to gain visibility. It requires manual review and almost certainly represents a non-legitimate company association.
